report_name: Revenue Mix (New vs Expansion vs Renewals)
primary_decisions_supported: Setting sales focus (new vs upsell vs retention)
Evaluating growth strategy (land-and-expand vs new logos)
executive_questions_answered: What portion of our revenue comes from new customers vs existing customers (expansion or renewals)?
Is our growth more driven by landing new clients or expanding existing ones?
Are renewals (existing recurring revenue) forming a healthy base of our revenue mix?

metric_definitions.definition: Annual recurring revenue from new customers (first-time bookings) in the period.
metric_definitions.formula: Sum of ARR from customers who signed in period (no prior ARR)
pitfalls: Definition of 'new' should exclude existing customer expansions – ensure proper flagging
A large enterprise initial deal might skew 'new' vs expansions in early years
Comparisons over time should account for stage of company (early-stage naturally high new logo mix, which shifts later)

metric_definitions.definition: ARR gained from existing customers expanding (upsells or cross-sells) during the period.
metric_definitions.formula: Sum of ARR increase for existing accounts (with prior ARR) in period
pitfalls: If customers downgrade and upgrade in same period, netting might hide true expansion activity
Highly usage-based models may blur line between expansion and renewals
Expansion mix tends to grow as company matures (later stage SaaS often 50%+ from expansion vs new)

metric_definitions.definition: ARR renewed from existing subscriptions (excluding any expansion portion) – essentially the retained recurring revenue.
metric_definitions.formula: Sum of ARR from contracts renewed in period (at same value as prior)

known_variants.definition_delta: Gross renewal = total up for renewal that renewed (ignores value change), Net renewal = value renewed including upsells/downgrades
known_variants.when_to_use: Separating pure retention from expansion effects

pitfalls: Renewal ARR can double-count expansion if not separated (best to treat expansion separately as above)
Including contraction (negative expansion) in renewals can confuse interpretation – track churn separately
Renewal rates may naturally decline as customer base diversifies – segment by cohort to get clarity

required_fields.field_name: Renewal Opportunity ID or Subscription ID

joins_and_keys.notes: Identify opportunities as new vs expansion by checking existing account ARR
Gather renewal info for existing contracts

transformation_rules: Mark opps as New Business if Account had no prior ARR (use Account creation or first deal date)
Renewals are usually identified via renewal opp or subscription term; ensure expansions are not double-counted as renewals
If a renewal includes an upsell, split the value into renewal (previous ARR) and expansion (incremental ARR) for clarity

data_quality_checks.logic: Every closed won deal is labeled correctly as New, Expansion, or Renewal (no undefined types)
Ensure each renewing contract is captured (e.g., no missed renewal opp if auto-renew etc.)

derived_fields: Total_New_ARR = sum(ARR of deals flagged New)
Total_Expansion_ARR = sum(ARR of deals flagged Expansion)
Total_Renewal_ARR = sum(prior ARR of renewed contracts)
aggregation_rules: Sum each category (New, Expansion, Renewal) for total company or by segment
Calculate percentages: New% = New_ARR / Total_ARR_in_period * 100, etc.
calculations_and_models.forecast_logic: Future mix can be projected by applying assumptions (e.g., expansion % increases as customer base grows) or using cohort trends

layout.panel_title: Revenue Mix % (New vs Expansion vs Renewal)
layout.purpose: Show proportion of revenue coming from new business, upsells, and renewals
See how revenue composition is shifting over time (e.g., expansion share growing)

narrative_insights_to_generate: Describe the current revenue mix and how it changed from prior year(s)
Note if expansion revenue is becoming a larger driver of growth as expected for a scaling SaaS
diagnostic_questions: Is the reliance on new business increasing or decreasing, and what does that imply for our sales strategy?
Are we generating enough expansion revenue from our customer base relative to industry benchmarks?

recommended_actions.action: Allocate more resources to upsell/cross-sell programs
recommended_actions.trigger_condition: Expansion ARR % is significantly below target or peer benchmarks

recommended_actions.action: Analyze new logo pipeline if new ARR contribution is declining
recommended_actions.trigger_condition: New ARR % drops for multiple periods (potential new business slowdown)

implementation_notes.minimum_viable_version: Basic split of total ARR this year into new vs existing customer sources
implementation_notes.advanced_version: Interactive mix analysis with toggle to include/exclude renewals vs expansion and scenario modeling for future mix (e.g., if retention improves)
common_client_customizations: Additional categories (e.g., Upsell vs Cross-sell split within Expansion)
Gross vs Net renewals as separate visuals
privacy_and_access_notes: No customer-level data, aggregate by category – safe for broad distribution
Be cautious sharing benchmarks externally; internal use to calibrate targets
success_criteria: Clear understanding across leadership of where growth is coming from (new vs existing business)
Targets set (for new sales vs upsells) align with observed mix and strategic goals
